commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair remains; the protected maintainer label means the remaining action is human review and merge judgment.; Cleared: The diff changes Android onboarding/auth flow but does not add supply-chain surface; scan auto-connect still uses existing setup-code validation and the normal saveGatewayConfigAndConnect path. commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Android onboarding so a successful Gateway setup QR scan resolves the setup code, saves the gateway connection config, starts pairing, and reuses the shared gateway config resolver with new unit coverage.

PR surface: Other +19. Total +19 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main's QR scan success listener only stores the scanned setup code, while the actual gateway connection starts only through Pair/Retry; I did not execute an emulator repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair remains; the protected maintainer label means the remaining action is human review and merge judgment.

target.title: fix: Android QR scan starts gateway pairing
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Android users scanning a valid Gateway setup QR code would return to Gateway Setup without starting the pairing connection.

The scanner accepted the QR code, but the app only copied the setup code into local UI state. Users then had to know to press Pair manually, so a successful scan appeared to do nothing.

## Why This Change Was Made

The scanner success path now resolves the scanned setup code into the same gateway connection config used by the Pair action, then starts the normal setup-auth connection flow and moves to Gateway Recovery. Onboarding now delegates setup/manual config resolution to the shared Android gateway config resolver instead of keeping a duplicate private resolver.

## User Impact

Android QR setup behaves like users expect: scanning a valid setup QR immediately starts gateway pairing and shows the recovery/approval state instead of silently staying on setup.

## Evidence

- Focused Android tests passed:
  - `./gradlew testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.ui.OnboardingFlowLogicTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.ui.GatewayConfigResolverTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.gateway.GatewaySessionInvokeTest --console=plain`
- Pre-commit autoreview passed clean:
  - `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- Live E2E proof after commit:
  - Installed committed Play debug APK into a Play-enabled Android emulator.
  - Started an isolated live OpenClaw gateway.
  - Scanned a real setup QR with Google Code Scanner.
  - App returned to `Gateway Recovery` with `Node Approval Pending` instead of remaining on `Gateway Setup`.
  - Gateway logs showed Android node connect with `auth=bootstrap-token`, followed by Android UI connect with `auth=device-token`.

AI-assisted: yes. I understand the code change and verified the user-facing QR pairing path live.
target.author: joshavant

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
